perturbation.original_text: iv) "Restricted Period" means during the Shareholder's employment with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates and continuing for a period of three (3) years following the date of termination, but in all events for a period of not less than five (5) years following the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: iv) "Restricted Period" means during the Shareholder's employment with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates and continuing for a period of one (1) year following the date of termination, but in all events for a period of not less than five (5) years following the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The 'Restricted Period' is initially defined as three years post-employment, but not less than five years from the agreement date. By changing the post-employment period to one year, while keeping the five-year minimum, this in-text contradiction makes it unclear what the actual restricted period is, and causes the contract to be contradictory, especially when enforcing section 1 b) IN GENERAL.
perturbation.contradicted_text: b) IN GENERAL. As a consequence of Shareholder's prior ownership and operation of Unitel, Inc. and employment with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, Shareholder has and will receive and deal with confidential information and business methods which are the exclusive property of the Company and the Company's Affiliates, including, but not limited to, its financial data, market data, business practices, pricing techniques, market development techniques, acquisition and development plans, and relationships with customers and suppliers. Shareholder further acknowledges that the confidential information acquired from the Merged Companies or the Company or the Company's Affiliates are of such a value and nature as to make it reasonable and necessary for the protection of the Company and the Company's Affiliates that Shareholder not Compete with the Company or the Company's Affiliates within the area and for a period of time hereinafter set forth, and that the Company and the Company's Affiliates will be irreparably injured, and the value of their capital stock and goodwill irreparably damaged, if Shareholder were to use or disclose any of the confidential information concerning the Company or the Company's Affiliates which Shareholder has acquired or will acquire, or if Shareholder were to Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates. Accordingly, except as hereinafter set forth, Shareholder represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that during the Restricted Period, neither Shareholder nor Shareholder's Affiliates will Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates in the Restricted Area. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing restrictive covenant, Shareholder further represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that neither Shareholder nor any of Shareholder's Affiliates will, during the Restricted Period: (i) promote the business of any person or entity engaged in a business which Competes with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates; (ii) solicit, divert or take away or attempt to solicit, divert or take away any of the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliate's customers, distributors, suppliers or patronage; (iii) attempt to seek or cause any of the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliates clients or customers to refrain from patronizing the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates; or (iv) employ or engage or attempt to employ or engage in any capacity any person employed or contracted by the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates at the date of termination of Shareholder's employment with the Company and any of the Company's Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'Restricted Period' from three years post-employment to one year, while maintaining a minimum of five years from the agreement date. This creates an in-text contradiction because the 'Restricted Period' is now ambiguously defined. The contradicted text, section b) IN GENERAL, relies on a clear definition of the 'Restricted Period' to enforce non-compete obligations. With the change, it's unclear whether the non-compete lasts for one year post-employment or a minimum of five years from the agreement, directly impacting the enforceability of the non-compete clause and triggering an inconsistency as per Perturbation Definition 5.

perturbation.original_text: Shareholder agrees that each of the covenants set forth in this Section 1 and in Section 2 is a separate and distinct covenant, independent of others and any other provision of this Agreement, and that the illegality or invalidity of any one, or more of the covenants or any part of one or more of them, will not render the others illegal or invalid.
perturbation.changed_text: Shareholder agrees that each of the covenants set forth in this Section 1 and in Section 2 is a separate and distinct covenant, independent of others and any other provision of this Agreement, except that the illegality or invalidity of the non-compete clause in Section 1 will render the confidentiality obligations in Section 2 also invalid.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the exception regarding the illegality or invalidity of the non-compete clause, it directly contradicts the first part of the sentence that these sections are independent of each other. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty and legal risk as the the severability of the contract become conflicting with each other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: e) SEVERABILITY. Shareholder agrees that each of the covenants set forth in this Section 1 and in Section 2 is a separate and distinct covenant, independent of others and any other provision of this Agreement, and that the illegality or invalidity of any one, or more of the covenants or any part of one or more of them, will not render the others illegal or invalid. If the invalidity or unenforceability is due to the unreasonableness of the time or geographical area covered by said covenants, then said covenants will nevertheless be enforced to the maximum extent permitted by law and effective for such period of time and for such area as may be determined to be reasonable by a court of competent jurisdiction, and the parties hereby consent and agree that such scope may be judicially modified in any proceeding brought to enforce such covenants.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the severability clause, stating that the invalidity of the non-compete clause in Section 1 will invalidate the confidentiality obligations in Section 2. This directly contradicts both the original text and the contradicted text (which are identical), which explicitly states that the illegality or invalidity of any one covenant will not render the others illegal or invalid. The added exception creates a clear conflict and in-text contradiction regarding the independence of the covenants, making the contract inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: The covenants set forth in this Section 1 and the covenants of confidentiality contained in Section 2 below are of the essence of this Agreement and will be construed as independent of any other of the provisions of this Agreement, the Merger Agreement and the Employment Agreement, and the existence of any claim or cause of action of Shareholder against the Company or the Company's Affiliates, whether predicated on this Agreement or otherwise, will not constitute a defense to the enforcement by the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates of any of said covenants.
perturbation.changed_text: The covenants set forth in this Section 1 and the covenants of confidentiality contained in Section 2 below are of the essence of this Agreement, however the existence of a claim or cause of action of Shareholder against the Company or the Company's Affiliates, whether predicated on this Agreement or otherwise, will constitute a defense to the enforcement by the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates of any of said covenants.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that any claim by the Shareholder does *not* affect the enforcement of the covenants. The modified text states the opposite: that a claim *will* constitute a defense. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty in how the agreement is to be interpreted and enforced, because the enforceability of the non-compete agreement is now dependent on whether a claim exists. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the Shareholder obligations in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: f) INDEPENDENT SIGNIFICANCE. The covenants set forth in this Section 1 and the covenants of confidentiality contained in Section 2 below are of the essence of this Agreement and will be construed as independent of any other of the provisions of this Agreement, the Merger Agreement and the Employment Agreement, and the existence of any claim or cause of action of Shareholder against the Company or the Company's Affiliates, whether predicated on this Agreement or otherwise, will not constitute a defense to the enforcement by the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates of any of said covenants.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that any claim by the Shareholder does NOT affect the enforcement of the covenants. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that a claim WILL constitute a defense, creating a strong and direct conflict within the document regarding the enforceability of the non-compete agreement. This directly contradicts section (f) where the original text existed.

perturbation.original_text: Upon demand by the Company, and in any event upon termination of Shareholder's services, Shareholder will deliver to the Company all original and facsimile records, documents and data in Shareholder's possession or under Shareholder's control, or in Shareholder's Affiliates' possession or under Shareholder's Affiliates' control, pertaining to the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon demand by the Company, Shareholder will deliver to the Company all original and facsimile records, documents and data in Shareholder's possession or under Shareholder's control, or in Shareholder's Affiliates' possession or under Shareholder's Affiliates' control, pertaining to the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates. Upon termination of Shareholder's services, Shareholder is not obligated to deliver any such records, documents, or data.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence, "Upon termination of Shareholder's services, Shareholder is not obligated to deliver any such records, documents, or data", creates a direct in-text contradiction with the initial sentence of the section that require the shareholder to turn over records upon demand and in any even upon termination. This directly contradicts the obligations of section 2 of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a) NON-DISCLOSURE - IN GENERAL. From the date hereof, Shareholder will maintain in strict confidence and will not, directly or indirectly, or through Shareholder's Affiliates, divulge, transmit, publish, release, or otherwise use of cause to be used in any manner contrary to the interests of the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, any confidential information relating to the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliates' systems, operations, products, services, business methods, management practices, contracts, computer programs and data bases, records, development data and reports, quality control specifications, cost analyses, flow charts, know-how, consumer lists, supplier lists, prospects, market development programs, acquisition programs, personnel data, or any information relating to customers, suppliers, products, sales, acquisitions, acquisition plans, financial structure, or pricing, and other information of like nature. Shareholder acknowledges that all information regarding the Company or the Company's Affiliates compiled or obtained by, or furnished to Shareholder regarding the Company, the Company's Affiliates or their respective businesses is confidential information and the Company's and the Company's Affiliates' exclusive property. Upon demand by the Company, and in any event upon termination of Shareholder's services, Shareholder will deliver to the Company all original and facsimile records, documents and data in Shareholder's possession or under Shareholder's control, or in Shareholder's Affiliates' possession or under Shareholder's Affiliates' control, pertaining to the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states that upon termination of services, the shareholder will deliver all records to the company. The changed text adds a sentence stating that upon termination of services, the shareholder is not obligated to deliver any such records. This creates a direct contradiction, as the shareholder is both required and not required to deliver records upon termination according to the modified clause, making it an inconsistency that leads to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, respecting the within subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, respecting the within subject matter, except for any side letters or oral agreements entered into prior to the date of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of "except for any side letters or oral agreements entered into prior to the date of this agreement" introduces a direct contradiction. The original clause is an integration clause designed to ensure that only the written agreement matters. By exempting prior side letters and oral agreements, the modified text now says that the agreement is *not* the entire agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction, since the contract says one thing but implies another thing, causing uncertainty on the shareholder and company to adhere to the full context of this contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c) ENTIRE AGREEMENT. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, respecting the within subject matter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original integration clause by adding an exception for side letters and oral agreements. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which is an identical integration clause stating that the written agreement is the entire agreement and supersedes all prior agreements. The addition makes the contract internally inconsistent, creating a clear in-text contradiction as the agreement states it contains the entire agreement and then introduces the possibility that other agreements outside of the contract also matter.
